name: Hash Map of Seen Values
summary: Scan once, storing each seen value's index, and look up the complement needed for the current number.
visualizationIntent: init: show an empty hash map and an empty result list. loop: visit each number from left to right and treat it as the current value. check: compute complement and look for it in the hash map of earlier values. update: if complement is absent, insert the current value with its index into the map. return: when complement is present, highlight the complement entry and return the stored index with the current index.

lineExplanations.3: Create an empty hash map that will store each previously seen number and its index.
lineExplanations.4: Create an empty result list that will be filled when the matching pair is found.
lineExplanations.5: Move through the array one index at a time.
lineExplanations.6: Read the current number at index i.
lineExplanations.7: Compute the value needed from an earlier index to reach the target with the current number.
lineExplanations.8: Check whether that needed complement has already been seen in the hash map.
lineExplanations.9: Build the result from the stored index of the complement and the current index i.
lineExplanations.10: Return the answer immediately after finding the matching pair.
lineExplanations.11: Store the current number and its index so a future number can pair with it.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def twoSum(self, nums, target): defines a method that receives the array nums and the integer target.
syntaxExplanations.3: num_map = {} creates an empty Python dictionary.
syntaxExplanations.4: result = [] creates an empty Python list.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(len(nums)): loops i over every valid index in nums.
syntaxExplanations.6: num = nums[i] reads the current array value.
syntaxExplanations.7: complement = target - num subtracts the current number from target to find the needed partner value.
syntaxExplanations.8: if complement in num_map: checks whether the dictionary already has complement as a key.
syntaxExplanations.9: result = [num_map[complement], i] builds a list using the saved complement index and the current index.
syntaxExplanations.10: return result sends the answer back and stops the method.
syntaxExplanations.11: num_map[num] = i stores or updates a dictionary entry whose key is num and whose value is i.
